title: Sean Penn Wins Best Supporting Actor Oscar for One Battle After Another
text: Sean Penn has secured his third Academy Award, winning Best Supporting Actor for his role in Paul Thomas Anderson's film One Battle After Another. The acclaimed actor, who portrays the menacing Colonel Steven J. Lockjaw, did not attend the ceremony. Kieran Culkin presented the award on Penn's behalf, stating: Sean Penn couldn't be here this evening — or didn't want to, so I'll be accepting the award on his behalf. According to the New York Times, Penn may have chosen to travel to Ukraine instead of attending the Oscars, continuing his long history of activism in the region where he co-directed the documentary Superpower in 2022. This win makes Penn the fourth male actor to achieve three Academy Awards. His previous Oscars both recognize Best Actor performances: one for Milk (2008) and another for Mystic River (2003). He also holds three additional Oscar nominations for Dead Man Walking, Sweet and Lowdown, and I Am Sam. Penn defeated fellow nominees Stellan Skarsgård, Delroy Lindo, Jacob Elordi, and his own co-star Benicio Del Toro. He had previously won the Actor Award and BAFTA for this role while declining to attend either ceremony.
